audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Thessalonians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for holiness, sexual ethics without body-hatred, sibling love, quiet work, resurrection comfort, Lord's arrival.

verses.restored: Finally then, siblings, we ask and appeal to you in Lord Jesus, that just as you received from us how it is necessary for you to walk and please the Divine, just as indeed you are walking, that you abound still more.
For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
For this is the will of the Divine, your sanctification: that you abstain from sexual immorality/misuse,
that each of you know how to possess one's own vessel/body in holiness and honor,
not in passion of desire like the nations who do not know the Divine,
that no one transgress and exploit one's sibling in the matter, because the Lord is avenger concerning all these things, just as also we told you before and solemnly testified.
For the Divine did not call us into uncleanness, but in holiness.
Therefore the one rejecting this does not reject a human but the Divine, the One giving his holy Spirit into you.
Now concerning sibling-love, you have no need for me to write to you, for you yourselves are taught by the Divine to love one another.
For indeed you are doing this toward all the siblings in all Macedonia. But we appeal to you, siblings, to abound still more,
and to aspire to be quiet, and to attend to your own things, and to work with your hands, just as we instructed you,
so that you may walk honorably toward those outside and have need of no one.
But we do not want you to be unaware, siblings, concerning those who sleep, so that you may not grieve as also the rest who have no hope.
For if we trust that Jesus died and rose, so also through Jesus the Divine will bring with him those who slept.
For this we say to you by word of the Lord, that we the living, those remaining until the presence/arrival of the Lord, will certainly not precede those who slept.
Because the Lord himself, with command, with voice of archangel and trumpet of Divine, will descend from heaven, and the dead in Messiah will rise first.
Then we the living, those remaining, will be caught up together with them in clouds into meeting of the Lord in air; and so we will always be with the Lord.
Therefore comfort/encourage one another with these words.

translation_notes: Sexual ethics are framed as holiness, honor, and non-exploitation, not body-hatred.
Sleep is used as death imagery; the point is hope and mutual comfort, not speculative timeline control.
Parousia is presence/arrival to preserve royal-visit sense.
